targets.negative_control_detail: diff_off_reproduced_collapse=true in 19:10Z (3/3 inert all gated arms) but FALSE in the byte-identical 20260518T063711Z run (0/3 inert) -- the collapse baseline is itself run-nondeterministic; this is the deeper tell, not a stable negative-control pass
targets.correction_rationale: byte-identical 06:37Z run landed branch (c) (all-escape, baseline reef 0.28); 19:10Z landed branch (e) (all-collapse, baseline reef 0.69). The two runs are opposite basins of an uncontrolled bistability (RNG/init + cross-run + cross-machine, continuous with 543g/543h). use_differential_heads does not control basin selection, so neither branch is attributable; the script's own branch-(c) non_contributory logic applies to the pair.

targets.recommended_evidence_quality_note: V3-EXQ-543i non_contributory all claims, both runs (failure autopsy 2026-05-19, user-confirmed). Two byte-identical runs landed opposite basins: 20260518T063711Z branch (c) -- diff-OFF gated arms 0/3 inert (no collapse baseline), all escape, baseline reef 0.28; 20260518T191052Z branch (e) -- diff-OFF 3/3 inert, diff-ON 3/3 inert, baseline reef 0.69. Basin selection is RNG/init + cross-run + cross-machine nondeterministic (continuous with 543g ACTIVE host-A / INERT cloud-3+cloud-4 and the 543h substrate-ceiling autopsy); NOT controlled by use_differential_heads, so ARC-062 could not express itself and the 19:10Z 'weakens' is a single-basin artifact. MECH-309's 19:10Z 'supports' rests on a collapse baseline that is itself non-deterministic -- flagged narrow/single-basin, not confirmation. ARC-062/MECH-309 have zero reliable contributory trained-policy evidence; pending_retest_after_substrate. INV-074 (universal invariant, lit_conf 0.82) and MECH-334 not weakened -- crystallization froze a collapsed/basin-dependent policy, mechanism never exercised; missing prerequisite, not claim pressure. Do NOT honor the 19:10Z per-claim weakens/supports in confidence or conflict scoring; do NOT honor 543i supersedes:V3-EXQ-543h as displacing any contributory result.

recommended_substrate_queue_entry.extends: 543h substrate_queue entry: GatedPolicy head-differentiation under outcome-coupled REINFORCE
recommended_substrate_queue_entry.metric_that_broke: n_inert_gating_seeds (per gated arm) -- AND its run-to-run nondeterminism
recommended_substrate_queue_entry.observed: byte-identical runs: 06:37Z all gated arms 0/3 inert (escape basin); 19:10Z all gated arms 3/3 inert (collapse basin). use_differential_heads (base +/- delta_hat, candidate-axis L2 pinned differential_bias_scale=0.1) did NOT make differentiation a strong attractor.
recommended_substrate_queue_entry.threshold_a_working_impl_must_exceed: n_inert_gating_seeds == 0 on ALL gated arms reproducibly across K>=3 repeated same-machine runs AND across >=2 machines; behavioural-divergence probe mean_tv_distance >= 0.05 sustained at end of P1; basin selection deterministic w.r.t. init RNG
recommended_substrate_queue_entry.root_cause_hypothesis: the differentiated-heads state is a flat/saddle equilibrium under outcome-coupled REINFORCE; head_init_bias_offset softmax-invisible, removed head_div term softmax-cancelable, disc_var@0.1 + delta_hat norm-pin insufficient to make the ACTIVE basin a strong attractor; basin chosen by uncontrolled nondeterminism
recommended_substrate_queue_entry.cross_machine_evidence: 543g 3-machine ACTIVE/INERT split; 543i adds cross-RUN ACTIVE/INERT split on a single host (06:37Z vs 19:10Z, byte-identical)
secondary_measurement_fixes: no per-claim evidence_direction emitted until basin stability shown across K repeated runs (determinism guard)

learning_extracted: use_differential_heads (delta_hat norm-pin) is insufficient to make head differentiation a strong attractor under outcome-coupled REINFORCE; basin-nondeterministic
bistability is cross-RUN on one host, not merely cross-machine; >=2-machine confirmation (543j) necessary but not sufficient -- basin determinism must be characterised over K repeated identical runs
per-claim evidence_direction must not be emitted from a single run when the script's documented failure mode is run-level basin nondeterminism

not governance-demotion (ARC-062 not tested fairly); not lit-pull (biology present, no divergence); not diagnose-errors (ran to completion)

cluster_pattern.shape: GatedPolicy head-differentiation does not robustly persist under outcome-coupled REINFORCE; gated arms collapse to inert (probe TV < 0.05) basin-nondeterministically. 543i: byte-identical runs landed opposite basins (06:37Z all-escape branch-c, 19:10Z all-collapse branch-e) -- the use_differential_heads structural fix did not convert differentiation into a strong attractor.
cluster_pattern.structural_property: the gated/rule-apprehension substrate has the asserted wiring but does not reliably carry differentiated policy behaviour under a trained policy; basin selection (ACTIVE/differentiated vs INERT/collapsed) is governed by uncontrolled nondeterminism (init RNG + cross-run + cross-machine), not by the experimental manipulation -- substrate-ceiling, unstable-activation variant, continuous with MECH-309's V_s-monostrategy substrate-ceiling
cluster_pattern.forced_decision: substrate reading confirmed and strengthened (cross-run on a single host) -> /implement-substrate; use_differential_heads marked insufficient; basin-determinism + cross-run threshold added; measurement/test-design fixes (manifest hostname, determinism-gate) folded into the next falsifier
cluster_pattern.cross_machine_proof: 543g 3-machine ACTIVE/INERT split; 543i cross-RUN ACTIVE/INERT split on a single host (byte-identical 06:37Z vs 19:10Z)
